title: Cold-install interpreter matrix never exercises ca-codex's differently-shaped hooks.json, and ca-codex's own mechanism drops STUB-python3 coverage
evidence: test_hooks_cold_install.py hardcodes `PLUGIN_ROOT = os.path.join(REPO, "plugins", "ca")` and `HOOKS_JSON = os.path.join(PLUGIN_ROOT, "hooks", "hooks.json")` (lines 68-69); no code path in the file references plugins/ca-codex. The ca plugin's hooks.json registers each event as TWO hook entries -- a primary `python3 ...` command and a runtime fallback `python3 -c "" || python ...` (e.g. hooks.json:6-9) -- which is exactly the REAL/STUB/NONE interpreter matrix the test proves. ca-codex's hooks.json instead uses ONE hook entry per event with a static `command`/`commandWindows` pair (e.g. hooks.json:6-10: `"command": "python3 ..."`, `"commandWindows": "python ..."`) selected by OS at parse time, not by runtime interpreter health -- there is no `python3 -c "" ||` runtime probe anywhere in ca-codex/hooks/hooks.json. The M2 plan itself flags this as an open exit criterion never closed: `.codearbiter/plans/codex-support.md:94` -- "cold-install matrix (test_hooks_cold_install.py REAL/STUB/NONE python) extended to ca-codex entries" -- listed as still-pending, and the current file confirms it was never done.
impact: Two independent gaps: (1) the matrix that exists specifically to catch a stock-Windows Store python3 alias (exits 9009 without launching) never runs against ca-codex at all, so a cold-install regression on the new host ships silently; (2) ca-codex's own single-entry commandWindows scheme has no runtime fallback for the STUB case -- on a Windows box where `python3` is a non-functional Store alias, ca-codex's Windows branch (`commandWindows: python ...`) is chosen unconditionally by OS, never probed, so if plain `python` is also absent/broken every hook silently fails to run (or the STUB python3 launches with wrong semantics) with no test ever proving otherwise.
recommendation: Extend test_hooks_cold_install.py to also run the REAL/STUB/NONE matrix against plugins/ca-codex/hooks/hooks.json, adapting the fallback-detection logic to ca-codex's command/commandWindows shape (or, if Codex's host schema truly has no runtime STUB-detection hook mechanism, document that limitation explicitly in docs/parity.md and decide whether ca-codex needs its own `python3 -c "" ||` runtime probe akin to ca's).
acceptance_criteria: test_hooks_cold_install.py (or a ca-codex-specific sibling test) exercises REAL/STUB/NONE python against plugins/ca-codex/hooks/hooks.json and is wired into CI
the STUB-python3-on-Windows case for ca-codex either has a proven runtime fallback or is documented as an accepted gap with rationale
